data.title: Bitcoin Holds $71,000 AsEthereum, XRP, Dogecoin Test Breakout Levels
data.description:
Bitcoin is holding near $71,000 as improving sentiment lifted crypto markets. Bitcoin ETFs saw $66.7 million in net outflows on Tuesday, while Ethereum ETFs reported $40.8 million in net outflows.  

Meme coin market capitalization rose 1.3% over the past 24 hours to $35 billion.

Trader Commentary:

Crypto chart analyst Ali Martinez said Bitcoin is approaching a breakout from a right-angled descending broadening wedge, with $75,700 as the next potential upside target if momentum holds.

For Ethereum, Martinez said the daily SuperTrend has flipped bullish for the first time since May last year. If support around $1,800 holds, it could mark the start of a new uptrend.

He added that Solana could climb toward $102 if it breaks above resistance near $95, signalling short-term continuation.

Analyst Cryptoinsightuk said XRP's liquidity setup favours upside, with roughly $310 million in short liquidations positioned above current levels compared with about $112 million below. The imbalance suggests a higher likelihood of a short squeeze.

Trader CW said Dogecoin is nearing a breakout from a six-month downtrend, which could signal the start of a broader uptrend if confirmed.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Google Accelerates Quantum Timeline: New Dual-Track Strategy Aims For 2030 Commercial Breakthrough
data.description:
Google Expands Dual-Track Quantum Strategy

On Tuesday, Alphabet's Google announced it is adding neutral atom technology to its existing superconducting approach to accelerate development and broaden real-world applications.

The company aims to combine the strengths of both systems—superconducting qubits for fast operations and neutral atoms for scaling to larger systems with flexible connectivity.

By investing in both approaches, Google plans to tackle different technical challenges in parallel and apply each method to specific use cases.

It is also building out a full research program focused on error correction, simulations, and hardware, while expanding leadership and partnerships to strengthen its quantum ecosystem.

UBS Highlights Opportunity—And Risk—In Quantum Stocks

In January, UBS analysts, led by Madeleine Jenkins, said that they see quantum computing as an early yet high-potential market, with applications across AI, cryptography, and simulation.

They expect "quantum advantage" to emerge in the 2030s as the technology matures.

Technical Analysis

Alphabet is trading 3.6% below its 20-day SMA and 5.3% below its 100-day SMA, which keeps the near-term trend under pressure even as the longer-term uptrend remains intact. Shares are up 70.29% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week high than their 52-week low.

RSI is at 33.21, which sits in neutral territory but is close to oversold, suggesting selling pressure may be getting tired. MACD is at -3.8752 and remains below its signal line at -3.4189, keeping bearish momentum in place despite the premarket lift.

The combination of RSI below 50 and a bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, with the bounce attempt still needing confirmation.

  • Key Resistance: $319.50
  • Key Support: $270.50

Alphabet Analyst Consensus

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $376.94. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $400.00) (March 13)
  • Wells Fargo: Upgraded to Overweight (Raises Target to $387.00) (February 23)
  • Tigress Financial: Strong Buy (Raises Target to $415.00) (February 19)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because GOOGL carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

GOOGL Price Action: Alphabet shares were up 1.31% at $294.25 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Chuck Schumer, Cory Booker Slam ICE Deployment, Urge Trump To Pay TSA Workers Now— 'Enough Is Enough'
data.description:
Democrats Demand TSA Pay, ICE Out Of Airports

On Tuesday, Schumer posted on X, "ICE needs to leave the airports NOW."

He added, "Trump needs to pay TSA workers NOW, and push Republicans to reach a deal. No intimidation forces at our airports. No more chaos at checkpoints. Enough is enough."

Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) said, "Donald Trump needs to pay TSA and get ICE the hell out of our airports," criticizing the deployment of ICE agents and urging immediate TSA payment.

Sen. Patty Murray (D-Wash.) said, "TSA agents don't need ICE officers hovering over their shoulders and intimidating travelers—they need to get paid!"

She noted that Democrats have tried eight times to fund TSA, but Republicans have blocked the efforts.

TSA Union Criticizes ICE Deployment

TSA union leaders criticized Trump's deployment of ICE officers to 14 airports, calling it an "insult" and a "waste of money" because the agents lacked training for passenger screening.

Hydrick Thomas, AFGE TSA Council 100 president, said the move placed a tactical force in a role needing specialized judgment, not just uniforms.

Last week, White House border czar Tom Homan clarified that ICE agents would not operate X-ray machines or screen passengers, instead handling tasks like monitoring exits to free TSA staff.

Trump praised the deployment and criticized Democrats for withholding funding, saying, "THEY WILL DO A FANTASTIC JOB. The great Tom Homan is in charge!!!"

Sen. Ted Cruz pushed to fund ICE and CBP separately from the DHS budget to bypass partisan gridlock.

He said, "What I’ve suggested is that the Democrats have gotten so extreme and unreasonable that I don’t know that they will ever be willing to fund Department of Homeland Security."

The partial government shutdown left TSA officers unpaid, increased absenteeism, and caused airport delays up to four hours during peak spring break travel.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Why Velo3D Shares Are Sliding Wednesday?
data.description:
Velo3D Inc (NASDAQ:VELO) shares are retreating Wednesday morning. This move follows the company’s fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results released Tuesday.

Velo3D reported fourth-quarter revenue of $9.44 million. This surpassed estimates of $8.68 million. The company posted an adjusted loss of 54 cents per share. This was slightly better than the 56-cent loss analysts expected.

3D printer and parts revenue decreased 5% year-over-year. Management cited product mix and system sales volume for the dip. System sales remain the primary 2026 revenue driver.

Growth Plans

The company's backlog reached $31 million by year-end. CEO Arun Jeldi noted a “record for bookings” in the quarter.

Jeldi envisions a plan for 400 production systems over the next decade. This plan remains “subject to securing additional financing.”

Short Interest Data

Benzinga data shows short interest in Velo3D recently decreased. It fell from 2.55 million to 2.31 million shares. Currently, 18.86% of the company’s publicly available shares are held short. It would take approximately 1.08 days for short sellers to cover their positions.

Technical Analysis

VELO is trading 1.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 11.2% above its 100-day SMA, a split that suggests short-term pressure while the longer-term uptrend is still technically intact.

Shares are up 302.38% over the past 12 months, and the stock is sitting closer to the middle of its 52-week range of $2.81 to $23.84 than to either extreme.

The RSI is at 51.41, which sits in neutral territory. Meanwhile, MACD is bullish with the MACD line at 0.2827 above the signal line at 0.2287.

  • Key Resistance: $13.50
  • Key Support: $12.50

VELO Stock Price Activity: Velo3D shares were down 7.54% at $12.50 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Michael Vi via Shutterstock

data.title: Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth To Lead Massive 'AI For Work' Takeover Across 78,000 Staff
data.description:
CTO Takes Lead On Companywide AI Push

Meta has put its chief technology officer, Andrew Bosworth, in charge of expanding AI adoption across its workforce as it pushes to stay competitive.

Bosworth will now lead the company's "AI For Work" initiative, taking over from Guy Rosen, as Meta works to move faster and operate more efficiently against smaller, AI-focused rivals.

In an internal note, he pointed to early pilots and rapid adoption of AI tools across teams as signs of growing momentum, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

Meta is driving AI use across its 78,000 employees to speed up workflows, simplify its structure, and reshape daily tasks.

CEO Mark Zuckerberg is also developing an AI agent to assist with his work, while employees are building and using tools like "MyClaw," which can access internal data and interact with colleagues.

Alongside this shift, Bosworth is overseeing a new AI-focused organization that supports teams developing large language models and aims to operate with leaner team structures.

He also emphasized the need to unify Meta's internal systems to ensure teams can build AI tools within a consistent framework.

Analyst Sees Long-Term Upside From AI Strategy

Rosenblatt analyst Barton Crockett remains bullish on Meta, maintaining a Buy rating with a $1,117 price forecast and highlighting its long-term AI positioning.

He views Meta's recent moves—such as its nuclear energy push, "Meta Compute" initiative, leadership changes, and job cuts in Reality Labs—as deliberate steps to support data center expansion and AGI ambitions. Crockett also points to potential growth in AR glasses and continued strength in advertising as key drivers.

While Meta plans heavy spending, he believes AI-driven revenue growth and improving AR momentum could help offset those concerns, leaving room for upside.

Technical Analysis

META is trading 5.7% below its 20-day SMA and 7.2% below its 100-day SMA, keeping the intermediate trend pointed down even as the stock attempts to base. Shares are down 5.33% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 34.52, in neutral territory but leaning toward "washed-out" conditions if selling pressure resumes. Meanwhile, MACD is at -14.8185 and remains below its signal line at -9.8841, a bearish configuration that suggests rallies may still face overhead pressure.

The combination of RSI in the 30–50 range and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $673.00
  • Key Support: $581.50

Meta Analyst Consensus

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $851.03. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Tigress Financial: Strong Buy (Raises Target to $945.00) (March 18)
  • Wells Fargo: Overweight (Raises Target to $856.00) (February 23)
  • Argus Research: Buy (Maintains Target to $800.00) (February 2)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because META carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

META Stock Price Activity: Meta Platforms shares were up 1.13% at $599.63 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock
